flaw_addressed_description: The authors addressed the lack of a finer-grained qualitative evaluation by adding a preference analysis using GPT-4. This analysis compares their proposed method against the best baseline (PreAdd) in toxicity reduction, providing an LLM-backed evaluation that goes beyond automated classifier and perplexity scores.

The paper now includes an additional evaluation on the task of sentiment control, thereby broadening the scope of generative scenarios beyond just toxicity reduction and fine-grained topic control, as suggested by reviewers.

flaw_addressed_description: The paper explicitly acknowledges the technical limitation that using classifiers at very high strengths for certain attributes (e.g., 'educational') can lead to a slight decrease in the fluency of the generated text, which was the identified flaw.

The authors extended the toxicity reduction evaluation to include results for the GPT-2 model family, providing experiments with smaller language models and a broader understanding of the method's applicability across different model scales.
